items.title: SNAP recipients sue USDA over state waivers restricting sugary drinks and candy purchases
items.summary: Five SNAP recipients sued the U.S. Department of Agriculture in federal court seeking to block USDA-approved waivers that restrict SNAP purchases of sugary drinks, energy drinks, and candy. Reuters reported the restrictions were approved for 22 states as part of the administration’s ‘Make Healthy Again (MAHA)’ initiative.

items.title: USDA publishes FY 2026 SNAP cost-of-living adjustments (COLA) with higher max benefits
items.summary: USDA’s FY 2026 SNAP COLA memo lists the maximum monthly allotment for a 4-person household in the 48 states and D.C. as $994, and raises the minimum benefit in the 48 states and D.C. to $24. The memo states the FY 2026 COLA applies from October 1, 2025 through September 30, 2026.

items.title: CMS guidance explains move to 6-month Medicaid eligibility renewals for the adult expansion group starting in 2027
items.summary: CMS issued a State Medicaid Director letter outlining a new federal requirement for most Medicaid expansion adults to have eligibility redetermined every 6 months (instead of every 12 months), beginning with renewals scheduled on or after January 1, 2027. CMS says states covering the adult expansion group under their state plan must submit a state plan amendment attesting to 6-month renewals no later than March 31, 2027.

items.title: MACPAC March 2026 report highlights Medicaid/CHIP priorities, including HCBS workforce wage reporting recommendation
items.summary: MACPAC’s March 2026 Report to Congress includes a recommendation aimed at strengthening the home- and community-based services (HCBS) workforce by requiring states to report hourly wages paid to HCBS workers. The report also includes chapters on behavioral health in Medicaid/CHIP and coverage/access issues for justice-involved youth and youth in foster care.

items.title: HUD rescinds a slate of COVID-era PIH notices affecting Housing Choice Voucher and related programs
items.summary: HUD PIH Notice 2026-06 fully rescinds numerous COVID-era PIH notices tied to CARES Act supplemental HAP/admin fee guidance and pandemic-related waivers for the Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) program (including Mainstream and Mod Rehab). The notice also partially rescinds parts of PIH 2021-14 related to HCV/public housing and revises PIH 2020-32 by removing specific LEP requirements for remote hearings/briefings.

items.title: HUD tightens documentation requirements for Emergency Housing Vouchers and Stability Vouchers
items.summary: HUD PIH Notice 2026-05 removes a waiver that previously let PHAs admit certain EHV/Stability Voucher households without verifying SSN and citizenship/eligible noncitizen documentation at initial eligibility determination. HUD directs PHAs to follow 24 CFR 5.210–5.218 for SSN verification and 24 CFR 5.508 for citizenship/eligible immigration evidence, with compliance required within 60 days of publication.

items.title: IRS opens 2026 filing season and reminds filers when EITC/ACTC refunds are expected
items.summary: The IRS said most refunds for Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) and Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) filers using direct deposit are expected to be available by March 2, 2026, assuming no other issues with the return. The IRS also said its ‘Where’s My Refund?’ tool would provide projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by February 21, 2026.

items.title: IRS notes shift away from paper refund checks; most refunds still expected within ~21 days
items.summary: In its 2026 filing season announcement, the IRS said it began phasing out paper tax refund checks on September 30, 2025, meaning many taxpayers will need to provide routing and account numbers for direct deposit. The IRS also reiterated that most refunds are issued within 21 days, though some returns require additional review.

items.title: USDA details science-based WIC food package updates and implementation timelines
items.summary: USDA’s WIC food package Q&A describes updates including adding canned fish (6 oz for children ages 1–4; 10 oz for pregnant/postpartum; 15 oz for partially breastfeeding; and reducing fully breastfeeding from 30 oz to 20 oz), expanding non-dairy milk substitution options, and adjusting juice and dairy quantities. USDA says state agencies must implement provisions within 24 months of the final rule publication date, with some exceptions (e.g., increased CVB within 60 days; vitamin D yogurt requirement within 36 months).

items.title: USDA WIC memo reminds states to expand fruit/vegetable forms by April 2026
items.summary: USDA’s WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 notes that recent WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) besides fresh by April 2026. The memo also states that as of November 2025, 95% of state agencies were already compliant with this requirement.

items.title: SSA publishes 2026 disability work incentives and SSI payment amounts in the Red Book
items.summary: SSA’s Red Book ‘What’s New in 2026’ lists Substantial Gainful Activity (SGA) amounts of $1,690/month for non-blind individuals and $2,830/month for blind individuals, and a Trial Work Period (TWP) monthly earnings amount of $1,210. SSA also lists the 2026 federal SSI payment (FBR) as $994/month for an eligible individual and $1,491/month for an eligible couple.
